observations.observation_value: Early beta2-adrenergic receptor agonist treatment drove microglia toward a homeostatic phenotype after SCI, reduced inhibitory extracellular-matrix deposition, remodeled the scar microenvironment, and supported reticulospinal axon regrowth/circuit reconstruction.

observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports microglial phenotype, ECM/scar remodeling, reticulospinal regeneration, and functional recovery endpoints
observations.notes: Full-text upgrade from PMID 41103249; inhibition/remodeling rather than simple depletion.

observations.outcome_type: Immune metabolism / lipid and debris handling
observations.observation_value: In female mouse spinal cord crush injury, TREM2 deletion and COG1410 activation experiments showed that TREM2 promotes myelin-debris clearance and disease-associated microglia activation but can also exacerbate chronic inflammation and fibrosis.
observations.normalized_value: TREM2 couples myelin debris clearance to chronic inflammation/fibrosis tradeoffs after SCI
observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports TREM2 expression, phagocytosis, foamy macrophages, DAM activation, axon regeneration, neuronal survival, BMS, and footprint outcomes
observations.notes: Full-text upgrade from PMID 41660680; contradiction boundary retained.

observations.observation_value: SCI-associated lactate accumulation was linked to H3K9 lactylation in lesion-infiltrating macrophages and circulating monocytes; integrated CUT&Tag/RNA-seq identified TXNIP as an H3K9la target that activates TXNIP-NLRP3 signaling and worsens inflammatory injury.
observations.normalized_value: lactate-H3K9 lactylation drives macrophage TXNIP/NLRP3 inflammation after SCI
observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports human serum lactate association, mouse SCI macrophage/monocyte H3K9la, multi-omics, and TXNIP/NLRP3 perturbation
observations.notes: Full-text upgrade from PMID 41856077; human/translational plus mouse mechanism.

observations.observation_value: Integrative bioinformatic and experimental analyses identified microglia as the predominant PANoptotic cell population after SCI; metformin-mediated AMPK activation suppressed microglial PANoptosis, shifted microglia toward repair-associated phenotypes, reduced inflammatory injury, and improved recovery endpoints.
observations.normalized_value: AMPK activation suppresses microglial PANoptosis after SCI
observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports bioinformatics, microglial cell-death specificity, metformin/AMPK perturbation, inflammation, tissue preservation, and functional outcomes
observations.notes: Full-text upgrade from PMID 42199531.

observations.observation_value: Integrated bulk RNA-seq and scRNA-seq analyses after SCI implicated activated microglia in PANoptosis activity and identified an IRF1-ZBP1 axis as a candidate driver of microglial pro-inflammatory cell death.
observations.normalized_value: multi-omics identifies IRF1-ZBP1 microglial PANoptosis after SCI
observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports cell-type PANoptosis scoring, candidate-gene prioritization, and therapeutic-target screening
observations.notes: Reclassified from generic polarization to programmed inflammatory death based on full text.

observations.observation_value: FGF4 was identified as a regulator of macrophage-mediated myelin-debris clearance; exogenous FGF4 activated FGFR1-PI3K/AKT signaling, upregulated Clec10a, enhanced intracellular myelin-debris processing, reduced neuroinflammation, and promoted spinal cord repair.

observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports in vitro debris phagocytosis, Clec10a perturbation, macrophage lipid/debris processing, inflammation, and repair endpoints

observations.observation_value: In a female rat T10 contusion-compression SCI model, systemic IL-4 treatment was tested as an anti-inflammatory/M2-polarizing therapy and was reported to improve functional recovery while reprogramming local and systemic neuroinflammatory molecular responses, with human SCI cytokine signatures examined for translational relevance.
observations.normalized_value: systemic IL-4 reprograms SCI neuroinflammation and supports functional recovery
observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports randomized rat SCI treatment groups, systemic IL-4 dosing, behavioral recovery, macrophage/microglia polarization, transcriptomic/cytokine analyses, and human translational signature comparison
observations.notes: Full-text upgrade from PMID 41799191; translational context retained.

author_claims.claim_text: Microglial depletion and CSF1R inhibition can reduce damaging inflammation in selected contexts but efficacy is timing-, sex-, and model-sensitive.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Negative or limited depletion studies are important boundary evidence against treating microglia removal as uniformly reparative.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Myeloid cells can organize wound containment or fibrotic scar programs, making broad suppression biologically unsafe.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Monocyte-derived macrophage lipid handling and macrophage-derived fibrotic signals connect immune state to tissue remodeling after SCI.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Multiple interventions report macrophage/microglia polarization shifts with functional or neuroprotective outcomes.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Polarization claims require marker-level extraction because drug, cytokine, and sensory-nerve programs may label different states with similar language.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Pyroptosis, PANoptosis, and NLRP3/inflammasome-linked programs form a distinct inflammatory injury family after SCI.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Programmed inflammatory death claims should be separated from generic inflammation and from apoptotic neuroprotection claims.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Immune-metabolic and debris-handling programs link myeloid state to repair, chronic inflammation, fibrosis, and synaptic integrity.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Myelin debris clearance can be reparative but may also coexist with chronic inflammation or fibrosis depending on pathway context.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Single-cell and spatial data provide state maps that should refine older bulk polarization categories.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Omics observations are mapping evidence unless directly tied to perturbation or functional outcomes.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Exosomes, hydrogels, nanoparticles, and vesicle platforms can reprogram immune environments but confound cargo, carrier, and timing effects.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Delivery systems should be tagged by immune target and payload rather than collapsed into a generic biomaterial category.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Module 3 evidence must be stratified by acute/subacute/chronic phase because the same myeloid population can be protective or harmful at different times.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Species and sex boundaries matter for immune modulation, especially where female-specific or zebrafish repair programs appear.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: The saturated Module 3A corpus supports mechanism-specific immune consensus profiles rather than a single anti-inflammatory therapy claim.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Repopulation after depletion is a distinct biological state and should not be treated as simple return to baseline microglia.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Plexin-B2 corralling establishes that immune-glial containment can support recovery.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: IL-4-type interventions can reprogram inflammatory responses but require species and route-specific interpretation.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Inflammasome activation can interact with systemic metabolic disease or small-molecule inhibition contexts.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: TREM2, FGF4/Clec10a, and debris-processing pathways should be curated as debris/efferocytosis mechanisms.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Nanoparticle and vesicle studies are high-value for therapeutic delivery but often abstract-level first pass until cargo and biodistribution are extracted.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Full-text upgrades should prioritize studies that contradict simple depletion, simple polarization, or simple anti-inflammatory interpretations.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Single-cell studies should be used to harmonize macrophage, microglia, monocyte, and neutrophil vocabularies across the database.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Macrophage-derived CXCL4 and TGF-beta scar programs connect immune state to Module 1 lesion architecture.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: IRF1-driven microglial PANoptosis is a candidate state program for future mechanistic extraction.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: SYK-dependent lipid handling and foam-cell C1q programs connect metabolic macrophage states to recovery and synaptic loss.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Localized cytokine delivery can improve repair outcomes while keeping systemic immune exposure lower in principle.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Module 3 consensus should remain mechanism-family based until full-text effect sizes and cell-marker panels are extracted.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Abnormally innervated sensory nerves may regulate macrophage polarization, linking immune state with circuit remodeling.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Microglial efferocytosis via LRP-1 is a candidate reparative clearance mechanism.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: OEC transplantation targeting macrophage extracellular traps cross-links immune modulation with Module 4 cell therapies.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Vascular-immune microenvironment reprogramming cross-links immune repair with scaffold and vascular modules.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Chronic nonresolving neuroinflammation is a separate target from acute protective immune activation.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Consensus wording should avoid treating inflammation as one directionally bad variable.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

boundary_records.interpretation_rule: Acute, delayed, and chronic immune modulation can produce opposite interpretations.
Myeloid corralling, wound compaction, debris clearance, and neutrophil-macrophage interactions can be reparative.
M1/M2 and pro/anti-inflammatory terms require marker, species, and timepoint extraction.
Pyroptosis, PANoptosis, inflammasome, and apoptosis-adjacent claims should not be collapsed into generic inflammation.
Hydrogels, vesicles, nanoparticles, and scaffolds can carry immune effects and material effects simultaneously.
Microglia, monocyte-derived macrophages, neutrophils, lymphocytes, and foam cells require separate ontology tags.
Immune mechanisms overlap with lesion scar architecture, cell transplantation, axon repair, and white matter repair.
Rows preserve first-pass evidence direction and topic placement, not quantitative effect sizes.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Microglial depletion and CSF1R-pathway modulation are timing-sensitive tools, not universal anti-inflammatory solutions.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Microglial depletion and CSF1R-pathway modulation are timing-sensitive tools, not universal anti-inflammatory solutions.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Myeloid cells can both contain wounds and drive fibrosis, so lesion remodeling claims must separate corralling from fibrotic scar programs.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Myeloid cells can both contain wounds and drive fibrosis, so lesion remodeling claims must separate corralling from fibrotic scar programs.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Macrophage/microglia polarization evidence is abundant but needs marker-level harmonization before high-confidence mechanistic pooling.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Macrophage/microglia polarization evidence is abundant but needs marker-level harmonization before high-confidence mechanistic pooling.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Inflammasome, pyroptosis, and PANoptosis pathways form a distinct inflammatory cell-death axis after SCI.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Inflammasome, pyroptosis, and PANoptosis pathways form a distinct inflammatory cell-death axis after SCI.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Immune metabolism, lipid handling, debris clearance, and efferocytosis connect myeloid state to repair and chronic degeneration.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Immune metabolism, lipid handling, debris clearance, and efferocytosis connect myeloid state to repair and chronic degeneration.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Single-cell and spatial studies should refine Module 3 vocabulary rather than serve as standalone therapeutic proof.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Single-cell and spatial studies should refine Module 3 vocabulary rather than serve as standalone therapeutic proof.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Immune-modifying delivery systems are promising but require careful separation of payload, carrier, route, and timing.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Immune-modifying delivery systems are promising but require careful separation of payload, carrier, route, and timing.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Module 3 supports mechanism-specific immune consensus profiles rather than a single claim that suppressing inflammation improves SCI.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Module 3 supports mechanism-specific immune consensus profiles rather than a single claim that suppressing inflammation improves SCI.
